title: Minecraft Earth Pigs
description: A resourcepack that adds all the variations of pigs from Minecraft Earth to the base game.
body:
![Banner Image](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a19cb25005521846974142a0fd671621f1502644.png)

Just like [My Other Resourcepacks](https://modrinth.com/user/AllSeeingEye_), this resourcepack adds mobs from the Minecraft spinoff games, to the base game. In this case its the pigs from Minecraft Earth!

Just like with the 1.21.5 update, these variants are biome dependant. Although they can also be forced by renaming a pig. IE. "Dried Muddy"


<details>
<summary>Spotted Pig</summary>
The spotted Pig has a 1 in 3 chance of spawning instead of a regular pig, in any biome.

![Spotted](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/41254118a6f936cfedca21219529284d36c5e9d2.png)

</details>

<details>
<summary>Piebald Pig</summary>
The Piebald Pig can be found in all of the badlands, Savanna and Desert biomes.

![Piebald](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/698d22f3710c4ea960b01475742955691b30c108.png)

</details>

<details>
<summary>Sooty Pig</summary>
The Sooty Pig, just like stated in the Minecraft Earth journal, can only be found in Dark Oak Forests.

![Sooty](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/db74e1b4ec26a9706a32080ebbef28a24c49923c.png)

</details>

<details>
<summary>Pink Footed- & Mottled Pig</summary>
Both of these variants have a 50/50 chance of spawning in any of the cold/frozen biomes.

![Mottled](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/fe7ec30c3f1dc3dfbe0697b056a124c8f1992c98.png)

![Pink Footed](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/41120a2ff2025108fa0de851a0fe8e92efef049a.png)

</details>

<details>
<summary>Muddy- & Dried Muddy Pig</summary>
Both of these pigs spawn exclusively in mangrove and regular swamp biomes. The Muddy pig has a 1 in 5 chance of spawning and the Dried Muddy a 4 in 5.

![Muddy](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0c74b8b0d90e2cd0244f7d4ccd54c2cb9d9ed73a.png)

![Dried Muddy](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/5a0cc9cd61d948503a1ad6ab341bc2dd5453cd56.png)

</details>
